channel.title: My Terminal Blog
channel.description: Recent content on My Terminal Blog

item.title: Day 2 – Ideas and Plan
item.description:
Today I outlined how I want to use this blog:

  • Journal for short daily notes about learning and progress
  • Projects for longer write-ups of software and hardware experiments
  • An About page that explains who I am and what I’m working on

Next steps: publish the site somewhere and automate deployments from my Git repo.

item.title: Terminal-Style Hugo Blog Setup
item.description:
This project documents how I created a terminal-style blog using Hugo and the Terminal theme.

Stack:

  • Hugo (extended)
  • Terminal theme by panr
  • Linux development environment

Milestones:

  1. Install Hugo and verify the extended build.
  2. Initialize a new site (hugo new site myblog).
  3. Add the Terminal theme under themes/terminal.
  4. Configure hugo.toml with the theme and menu.
  5. Create journal and projects sections with sample content.

item.title: Day 1 – Getting Started
item.description:
Today I set up my new Hugo site using the Terminal theme on my Linux machine.

The main goals were:

  • Install Hugo (extended)
  • Create a new blog project
  • Wire up the Terminal theme
  • Prepare sections for journal entries and project updates

Everything is running locally, and I can already see the retro terminal styling on the homepage.

About Me

Eagle Dad / Girl Dad / First Responder / Hacker Nerd / Aspiring Polymath

Open Source, Linux, Terminal, Bash, Tmux, and NeoVim, are the way. My homelab is my safe space.

This is my attempt at a blog to share what I learn with anyone who may be interested.

If you’re nerd and have questions, ideas, or just want to connect, contact me on LinkedIn!

I enjoy using technology to solve complex problems. I explore and discover new tips tools and techniques. I record the struggled and successes of my efforts in notes or wiki pages. Building on previous efforts and sharing with others is very rewarding.
